# The Meteorite Illusion: Exposing Human Bias in the Search for Cosmic Treasure

## Overview 

This documentation describes the conception, development and evaluation of a data visualisation product. A socio-economic, geographic and economic analysis of meteorite discoveries worldwide. NASA records where meteorites were found, but this data is heavily biased by human presence and environmental conditions.

## Main goal: 
- Deconstruct the “Discovery Gap”: show that meteorite-finding maps are largely maps of human activity and geography.
- Correlate NASA finds with population density (human presence), aridity (preservation potential) and GDP per capita (economic context).
- Integrate a market-value estimation based on meteorite classification (recclass) and mass: a “Fair Value” benchmark.
- Optional milestone: Shift from a map of findings to an interactive Opportunity & Value Map that protects collectors from fraud and helps explorers locate high-potential, unrecorded search zones.

![The vizualization product development process](pics/vizproductprocess.png){#fig-vizprocess}

The methodological procedure of this work follows the process model shown in @fig-vizprocess, which is based on the concepts on data product development outlined in @DoemerKempf2022_1000150238 and the [data science project-template](https://github.com/phonosync/dataviz-project-template) on github.

It comprises the following four main phases with the corresponding documentation artefacts:

* **Project Understanding** &rarr; [Project Charta](project_charta.qmd): Context analysis, stakeholder and user analysis (based on the Value Proposition Canvas), formulation of objectives, success criteria, constraints and the visualization concept.
* **Data Acquisition and Exploration** &rarr; [Data Report](data_report.qmd): Identification and acquisition of relevant data sources, followed by exploratory data analysis to understand data quality and structure.
* **Visual Encoding and Design** &rarr; [Visualization Design Report](viz_design_report.qmd): Documentation of the data-to-visual mapping, layout and composition, interaction design, visual style and the resulting prototype(s).
